properties.remark: AWOS STATION KPOY POWELL AIRPORT. WIND GUSTS WERE ASSOCIATED WITH VICINITY THUNDERSTORMS ALONG A STRONG COLD FRONT.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL.

Delayed Report. Damage to a barn and several outbuildings from a strong thunderstorm wind gust at a ranch between Thermopolis and Kirby WY. Estimated wind gust of 50 to 60 mph based on damage. Damage estimated to have occurred around 426pm MDT June 4 2023 based on radar. Nearest surface ob was a personal weather sensor 4 miles away, which showed a gust to 22 mph at the 445 pm reporting time, but storm did not directly affect this sensor location. Sheds, items, and debris were blown east to west generally with no indication of distinct rotation. Outbuildings had openings to the east, making them especially susceptible to easterly wind gusts. Little to no damage to nearby farms or trees, as the downdraft was from a small storm, leading to very localized damage, likely from an individual downdraft.
Part of Interstate 25 is shut down due to flooding. 10+ streets in downtown Casper are flooded and closed. Many streets in low-lying areas around Casper are flooding due to intense rain rates.
Emergency manager has reported cars floating down downtown streets along with a home wiped off its foundation.
ASOS station KCPR Casper Airport.

Delayed report. Social media picture showing large cone tornado on the ground looking southwest from Edgerton. Estimated location based on radar rotation, time of picture, and picture angle.

Delayed report. Damage to a residence between mile marker 21 and 22 on WY HWY 259. Hail estimated at 2.5 inch dimeter based on picture. Horse trailer flipped, fuel tanks blown away and missing, and trees damaged.

Video shared of rural dirt road washout in Brown Draw, due to high flow from heavy thunderstorm rain upstream, which is south, of this location. According to spotter, this road has been washed out multiple times in recent history, and it is unclear if there is any culvert or drainage below road surface.
